message:
fix(core,vdbe,func): truthiness uses float comparison, strftime %J trailing zeros

Fix #76: ALL C SQLite truthiness checks use sqlite3VdbeRealValue() != 0.0,
not integer truncation. 0.5 AND 1 = 1, NOT 0.5 = 0, IIF(0.5,...) = truthy.
Verified by oracle. Fixed in connection.rs (is_sqlite_truthy), engine.rs
(vdbe_real_is_truthy, OP_If/IfNot/Not/IsTrue, sql_and/sql_or), and
builtins.rs (IifFunc).

Fix #77: strftime('%J',...) was using fixed 6 decimal places. C SQLite
uses %.15g which strips trailing zeros. Fixed in datetime.rs.

Added 16 conformance oracle test suites (~150+ queries): window functions,
correlated subqueries, string edge cases, UPDATE patterns, aggregates,
CASE expressions, multi-table DML, type coercion, truthiness edges,
LIMIT/OFFSET, datetime modifiers, JOIN edge cases.

1916 core tests pass, 0 regressions. Clippy + fmt clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
